utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 7 items in the required format: destination, travel_dates, transportation, accommodation, activities, additional_services, total_budget. The final package includes entries for all 7 items. Now verify if each is fully specified with required details: 1) destination: provided, includes two cities—acceptable. 2) travel_dates: specific dates provided—OK. 3) transportation: requires flight details with costs. Provided: 'Flights (Budget Airlines, Economy Class) for 4 people (round trip to Prague via London). Cost: €600.' Lacks specific flight details (airline names, flight numbers, departure/arrival times, origin city); only general 'Budget Airlines'—not fully specified. 4) accommodation: requires hotel selection with costs. Provided hotel names, nights, per-night rates, and totals—OK. 5) activities: selected activities with costs. Provided only estimated totals per city, no specific activities—insufficient. 6) additional_services: any extra services with costs. Provided as 'None'—this is acceptable as a valid specification (no extras). 7) total_budget: complete cost breakdown. A single total (€1645) is given, but not a full breakdown beyond line items elsewhere; however, since accommodation and transportation costs are itemized and activities are estimated, the total is consistent—acceptable as a total budget figure. Therefore, fully specified items are: destination, travel_dates, accommodation, additional_services, total_budget = 5/7.
